issues.unsupported_claim: r-selected examples listed as 'insects, weeds, fish' in the recap card; specific_examples line 'r-selected species — insects, weeds, and many fish'
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: The reference says r-selected examples are 'insects, weeds, many fish' — fully supported. The recap shortening to 'fish' (instead of 'many fish') is the only deviation and is trivial; all species named (insects, weeds, fish, humans, elephants, large mammals) ARE in the reference.
issues.suggested_fix: Keep 'many fish' wording for fidelity to the reference; no factual change needed.

issues.unsupported_claim: Type I 'humans and large mammals', Type II 'some birds, small mammals, reptiles', Type III 'oysters, many fish, plants'
issues.where_in_reference_it_is_missing: All three survivorship example sets match the reference exactly (Type I humans/large mammals; Type II some birds/small mammals/reptiles; Type III oysters/many fish/plants). No fabrication.

overall_assessment: The outline is highly faithful to the reference. The exam-percentage claim ('10–15% of AP exam' for Unit 8) matches the reference exactly and appears consistently (cb_unit, 01_title, 15_energy_preview). Every formula is verbatim: r = (b − d) + (i − e), dN/dt = rN, dN/dt = rN(K − N)/K, and doubling time ≈ 70/r — all present in the reference. No fabricated dates, no invented researcher names, no Latin binomials, no out-of-reference numeric examples. All species named (insects, weeds, many fish, humans, elephants, large mammals, oysters, plants, some birds, small mammals, reptiles) come directly from the reference lists; no novel species were introduced. The dispersion claims (clumped most common/patchy, uniform/territorial, random rare), density-dependent and density-independent factor lists, and the 'spectrum not binary' framing all match. The 'matter cycles, energy flows / lost as heat' preview (15_energy_preview, 8.2) and the 'K is a leveling-off point, populations can overshoot' nuance are NOT stated in the reference text — they are reasonable AP-standard generalizations but technically unsupported; flagged as low-risk because they assert no specific number/date/name. References to internal logistics (GIIS Unit 8 problem set, advisor check-in, Module 13/14 sequencing) are course-structure metadata, not factual claims about ecology, so they are out of scope for citation checking. No critical hallucinations found.
